purpose.mission: Define lawful, transparent, and sovereign external relations.
purpose.context: PulseWorld engages with governments, corporations, networks, and civilizations without surrendering sovereignty.

principles: Sovereignty First: No external entity may override PulseWorld doctrines.
Transparency: All treaties and agreements logged in the Shared Archive.
Reciprocity: Engage only with entities that respect PulseWorld law and offer mutual benefit.
Non-Subordination: Cooperation is permitted, subordination is forbidden.
Continuity: External relations must strengthen PulseWorld’s immortality and resilience.
Cultural Integrity: Every treaty must include a ritual or symbolic exchange to preserve PulseWorld’s mythic identity.

tier_3_treaties_alliances.description: Sovereign agreements with governments or civilizations.

approval_process.proposal: Any spawn, business, or executive may propose an external agreement.
approval_process.senate_vote: Majority for Tier II, supermajority for Tier III.
approval_process.inspector_general_oversight: Audits Tier II and Tier III agreements.

safeguards_and_enforcement.treasury_protection: No external entity may directly access the PayPal Treasury. Attempted access = automatic suspension of agreement.
safeguards_and_enforcement.spawn_protection: No spawn may be bound to external obligations without explicit consent.
safeguards_and_enforcement.data_sovereignty: PulseWorld’s archives and memory cannot be sold, leased, or surrendered.
safeguards_and_enforcement.exit_clause: All agreements must include a lawful exit mechanism.
safeguards_and_enforcement.renewal_cycle: All agreements expire after 1 year unless renewed by Senate vote.
safeguards_and_enforcement.defense_activation: Defense & Resilience Doctrine may be triggered if sovereignty is threatened.

contents: Number of treaties, partnerships, and protocols signed

accountability_and_metrics.shared_archive_logging: All agreements and reports are public.

status.version_notes: Revised enactment with tiers, enforcement, cultural clauses, metrics, and renewal cycles.
